title: Repository Purpose, Risk Profile, and Definition of Done · Policy Workshop
summary: Design quality gates for a new repository according to its actual failure costs. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a repository engineering contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.
Rank correctness, security, accessibility, performance, compatibility, and maintenance risks.
Define machine-verifiable and human-reviewed completion criteria.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a gates-exception-policy-7gk45a: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Exception Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-exception-policy-7gk45a:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

stimulus.title: Exception Policy failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A disabled rule needs no explanation if CI passes.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence.

options.text: Keep the plausible Exception Policy output and hide the violated a repository engineering contract assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Exception Policy without its stated preconditions, omit the a repository engineering contract trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Exception Policy for a repository engineering contract.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Exception Policy condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the workshop evidence note for Exception Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed workshop case for gates risk contract that tests this competency: Document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Exception Policy through the concrete a repository engineering contract case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence.
